questions.question: What is Git?
options: A version control system that tracks changes in files
A database management system for storing data
A programming language for writing code
A web browser for accessing websites
questions.answer: A version control system that tracks changes in files
questions.explanation: Git is used to track changes in files and manage versions.

questions.question: What command is used to initialize a Git repository?
questions.explanation: This creates a hidden .git directory that stores, commits, history, branches and configurations.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the '.git' directory?
questions.explanation: The .git directory stores, commits, history, branches and configurations.

questions.question: What is the 'Working Directory'?
options: The area where Git prepares changes for commit
This is where we create and edit our files
A snapshot of our project at a moment
The area where we stage files
questions.answer: This is where we create and edit our files
questions.explanation: The Working Directory means this is were we create and edit our files.

questions.question: What command stages the file for commit?
questions.explanation: The git add command just stages the file to commit but it does not create a commit.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Staging Area'?
options: This is where preparation is done
A snapshot of our project at a moment
The area where we stage files
questions.answer: This is where preparation is done
questions.explanation: The Staging Area this is where preparation is done.

questions.question: What command commits the changes?
questions.explanation: The git commit command creates a snapshot of our project at this moment.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Commit History'?
options: A snapshot of our project at a moment
This is where we stage files
The area where we create and edit our files
questions.answer: A snapshot of our project at a moment
questions.explanation: The Commit History means this is the first snapshot that is why it says initial commit.

questions.question: What command pushes the changes to GitHub?
questions.explanation: The git push command pushes the changes to GitHub.

questions.question: Where can you view the file that has been pushed to GitHub?
options: On the internet in your github profile
questions.answer: On the internet in your github profile
questions.explanation: You can view the file that has been pushed to GitHub by going to your github profile.
